corrections.text: The article places the phrase "will observe around 100 million stars" inside quotation marks. The cited NASA Goddard release (via ScienceDaily) actually reads that Roman's galactic bulge survey "will observe approximately 100 million stars." The figure and meaning are unchanged; the word 'around' should read 'approximately' inside the quotation marks.

The article quotes the telescope traveling "via the Pegasus barge from Goddard Space Flight Center in Maryland." NASA's media advisory does not use that exact wording; it states the observatory "will arrive aboard NASA's Pegasus barge from NASA's Goddard Space Flight Center in Greenbelt, Maryland." The transport facts (Pegasus barge, from Goddard in Maryland) are accurate, but the quoted span is a paraphrase rather than a verbatim quotation.
